secondary: Outer city mirrored back into mind.
Nested layers of experiencer and dream.
States arrayed as phases of one awareness.
The world as persuasive vibration of thought.
Radial map of nine aesthetic flavors.
Close physical eyes, open the other ones.
Mātṛkā as the field of letters.
Syllables appear as stations in the body.
Listening inverts the direction of sound.
Time emerges as the soul’s extension.
Ascent is a reversal of attention.
Recognition begins as remembering rather than acquisition.
The witness cannot be objectified from outside.
Prakāśa and Vimarśa as inseparable aspects.
Error hides reality without destroying it.
Liberation changes the seeing, not the world.
Symbols participate rather than merely signify.
The vessel is prepared for descent.
Names and constellations as operative correspondences.

scenes.title: Three doors, one room
scenes.meaning: Three means lead toward one reality.

scenes.title: The three means
scenes.meaning: Three paths distinguished by operative power.

scenes.title: The first instant
scenes.meaning: Śāmbhavopāya is represented by the preconceptual flash.

scenes.title: No means
scenes.meaning: All means resolve into the absence of separation.

scenes.title: Twenty-seven varieties
scenes.meaning: The larger graded spectrum is hinted through a twelve-node cycle.

scenes.title: The unclenching heart
scenes.meaning: Ignorance as contracted awareness; grace as release.

scenes.title: Effortlessness
scenes.meaning: The highest grade culminates in spontaneous revelation.

scenes.title: The mirror generates images
scenes.meaning: Pratibimbavāda begins with a self-manifesting mirror.

scenes.title: No external original
scenes.meaning: Unlike an ordinary mirror, consciousness does not copy an external original.

scenes.title: Fifty reflective powers
scenes.meaning: Reflective awareness proliferates as articulated powers.

scenes.title: Every tattva as mirror-face
scenes.meaning: The doctrine can be applied across the tattvas.

scenes.title: Pot becomes shards
scenes.meaning: Transformation without a hidden substance behind appearance.

scenes.title: Five people, one moon
scenes.meaning: Shared cognition without requiring an independent object behind appearance.

scenes.title: Touch the mirror
scenes.meaning: The practical consequence is transformation rather than escape.

scenes.title: Power predominance
scenes.meaning: Correspondence between powers and pure tattvas.

scenes.title: Sketch to painting
scenes.meaning: Sadāśiva and Īśvara as different emphases.

scenes.title: Beyond the map
scenes.meaning: The pure hierarchy opens toward Anuttara.

scenes.title: The wheel of twelve
scenes.meaning: The twelve Kālīs as a wheel of consciousness.

scenes.title: Five acts in one perception
scenes.meaning: The five acts as ongoing activities of awareness.

scenes.title: A thought appears
scenes.meaning: The first three acts in an individual cognition.

scenes.title: The limited Śiva
scenes.meaning: The individual enacts the same fivefold power in contracted form.

scenes.title: Descent of speech
scenes.meaning: The four levels in descending order.

scenes.title: Three impurities
scenes.meaning: The three malas as three modes of contraction.

scenes.title: The three shells
scenes.meaning: The three malas as concentric shells.

scenes.title: Āṇava mala
scenes.meaning: Āṇava mala as finitude or incompleteness.

scenes.title: Kārma mala
scenes.meaning: Kārma mala as bounded agency and karmic consequence.

scenes.title: Means cut the shells
scenes.meaning: The common pedagogical mapping of malas to upāyas.

scenes.title: Subjective and objective
scenes.meaning: Three vācaka and three vācya paths.

scenes.title: One object in six paths
scenes.meaning: A simple object occupies both expressive and objective paths.

scenes.title: Discard the path
scenes.meaning: The sixfold path is ultimately transcended.

scenes.title: The Māyā Perceiver
scenes.generator: Voronoi field with central blind spot
